entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: The crop being studied for its popping and flaking quality

entities.name: POPPING QUALITY
entities.description: The quality of sorghum grain when subjected to popping

entities.name: FLAKING QUALITY
entities.description: The quality of sorghum grain when subjected to flaking

entities.name: PHYSICOCHEMICAL CHARACTERISTICS
entities.description: The physical and chemical properties of the grain

entities.name: STARCH DIGESTIBILITY
entities.description: The measure of how easily starch in the grain is broken down in vitro

entities.name: PROTEIN DIGESTIBILITY
entities.description: The measure of how easily protein in the grain is broken down in vitro

entities.name: POPPING
entities.description: A heat treatment process used to expand sorghum grains

entities.name: FLAKING
entities.description: A heat and pressure process used to flatten sorghum grains

entities.name: HEAT PROCESSING
entities.description: The general application of heat to grain, encompassing popping and flaking

relationships.description: Sorghum cultivars exhibit significant genotypic differences in popping quality
Sorghum cultivars exhibit significant genotypic differences in flaking quality
Sorghum cultivars show variation in in vitro starch digestibility
Sorghum cultivars show variation in in vitro protein digestibility
Popping causes a five-fold increase in starch digestibility
Popping causes a shift and deleterious effect on protein digestibility
Flaking causes a five-fold increase in starch digestibility
Flaking causes a shift and a more pronounced deleterious effect on protein digestibility
Heat processing has a deleterious effect on protein digestibility
Popping is a type of heat processing
Flaking is a type of heat processing
Popping quality is related to the physicochemical characteristics of the grain
Flaking quality is related to the physicochemical characteristics of the grain
